codeReview.summary: The PolicySelector component is well-implemented with clean React patterns, comprehensive test coverage (27 tests), and proper integration with the AppContext. The 'Red Moment' feature (No Insurance mode showing all items as red with 100% gap) is correctly implemented. The component supports both compact and full variants, has excellent accessibility (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation), and includes localStorage persistence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly. Used TDD approach, ran tests successfully, and properly integrated with existing AppContext. The handoff shows proper use of vitest, React Testing Library, and the established mocking patterns for coverageLookup.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: Created test file first with proper vitest mocks, then implemented component, ran tests with 'npm test -- src/components/PolicySelector/' achieving 27 passing tests

sharedStateObservations.observation: The component follows established project patterns: uses AppContext for state management, integrates with coverageLookup utility, uses Tailwind CSS for styling, and follows the component structure used by Dashboard and CoverageOverlay components. All 4 policy types (renters, homeowners, auto, none) are properly handled.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: PolicySelector.jsx imports useAppContext from './context/AppContext.jsx', uses lookupCoverage from '@/utils/coverageLookup.js', and follows the same export pattern with index.js barrel file as other components

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services were started. The worker correctly used existing 'npm test' and 'npm run dev' commands already documented in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'npm test -- src/components/PolicySelector/' and 'npm run dev' - both standard commands

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that CoverageOverlay has pre-existing canvas-related test failures unrelated to PolicySelector. This knowledge was properly recorded in the handoff's discoveredIssues. The worker also correctly handled the vitest jsdom environment's localStorage mocking requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues: 'Some pre-existing CoverageOverlay canvas tests fail due to jsdom limitations with canvas elements. These are not related to PolicySelector implementation.' Worker implemented proper localStorage mock: 'const localStorageMock = { getItem: vi.fn(), setItem: vi.fn(), ... }'

summary: PolicySelector feature successfully implemented with high-quality code. Component includes 4 policy options with proper 'Red Moment' functionality, 27 comprehensive tests covering all requirements from validation-contract.md (VAL-PS-001 through VAL-PS-005, VAL-OVERLAY-012, VAL-DASH-008, VAL-CROSS-003, VAL-CROSS-011). Properly integrated into App.jsx header with compact variant. All PolicySelector tests pass. No blocking issues found.
